##NAMES=Whitlockite
##RRUFFID=R080052
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_9_Mg(PO_3_OH)(PO_4_)_6_
##LOCALITY=Palermo #1 Quarry, Groton, Grafton County, New Hampshire USA
##OWNER=Harvard
##SOURCE=Mineralogical Museum, Harvard University
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless granular massive, and as rhombohedra in cavities, associated with white rice-shaped fluorapatite, and colorless quartz. Type sample.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R080052
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Ca_8.81_Na_0.18_Sr_0.01_)(Mg_0.62_Fe^3+^_0.34_Mn_0.02_Al_0.02_)(P_1.00_O_4_)_6_(P_1.02_O_3_OH)
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 10.359 b: 10.359 c: 37.086 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 120 volume: 3446.5 crystal system: hexagonal
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
